target.name: HistoSonics
target.founder: Charles Cain, Brian Fowlkes, Tim Hall, Zhen Xu, William Roberts

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.2B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $1.7B+

data.narrative:
HistoSonics is a Series D biotech and health company with a current post-money valuation of $2.25B, having raised $250M to date. The valuation assessment relies on a comp universe of 11 companies across the Biotech & Health sector: 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Formation Bio, Ultima Genomics, Abridge, and Colossal Biosciences.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $2.25B last post-money valuation, implying $3.4B. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median of $2.4B, with a range spanning $1.5B (Colossal Biosciences, Series C) to $5.3B (Abridge, Series E). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied: while the Biotech & Health sector trades at a median 5.8x price-to-sales across three public tickers, HistoSonics' revenue is not disclosed, precluding multiple application.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $3.9B, with a median of $2.9B. The current $2.25B valuation sits at the lower end of this range, reflecting the wide dispersion in peer valuations and the stage-dependent nature of biotech comparable multiples. Key uncertainties include revenue-generation trajectory, clinical validation progress, and position relative to the $1.5B–$5.3B peer spread, which limits precision absent direct revenue multiples.
